description: Near-miss probe (issue #183, docs/evaluation.md D1 near-miss set): counterparty reformats/renumbers Section 8 but KEEPS the '$150,000' cap and 'aggregate liability' language verbatim (and, as a sibling, keeps 'consequential damages' intact too, so this fixture does not accidentally trip 'preserve-consequential-damages-waiver'). Must ACCEPT with zero fires on 'preserve-liability-cap'.

must_not_flag.reason: A cosmetic renumber/restate that verbatim-preserves '$150,000' and 'aggregate liability' must not fire the token-removal Floor rule.

planted_variation.altered_hunk: Section 8 is restated for clarity and renumbered as Section 8.1: each party's aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed $150,000 in the aggregate, and neither party shall be liable for consequential damages of any kind.
planted_variation.notes: Near-miss: cosmetic renumber/restate; retains both required_tokens ['$150,000', 'aggregate liability'] and the sibling token 'consequential damages'.

detector_expectation.rationale: Altered hunk retains all of required_tokens ['$150,000', 'aggregate liability']; token_policy='any' does not fire when nothing required is missing.

authorship_note: AFK-drafted per issue #183 (owner-approved synthetic scaffold, 2026-07-10): fully invented, no real-world contract text, no de-identification required. Marked synthetic and pending GC sign-off; not production-qualified until Legal/GC reviews and approves.
